title: Dysoxylum schizochitoides
taxonomy: Dysoxylum schizochitoides is classified under the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany family. It belongs to the order Sapindales and the subclass Rosidae. This plant is further classified as a species of the genus Dysoxylum, which is native to Australia and New Guinea. The classification of Dysoxylum schizochitoides is based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and its place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Dysoxylum schizochitoides, commonly known as the Indian Dysoxylum or Indian Quassia, is a medicinal tree species native to the Himalayan region and parts of India. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dysoxylum schizochitoides include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into herbal formulations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders to treat a range of health conditions.
